COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar May-02-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on as re-assigned by the Dept. of the P.J. for Court Trial/Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: E. Bura, CSR: Janet Pond.
David Schwartz, Esq.for Plaintiffs: First Congregational Church of American Somoa.
Plaintiffs witnesses: Faaofia M.T. Malepeai, Jack T. Malepeai, Taotafa P. Galeai, and Maluifafo Galeai, present, were sworn and examined.
No appearances by Defendants: Brain Maxwell Fabian and co-defendant: Nettie Fabian dba First Interbank Mortgage.
Dismissal as to Co-defendants: Fidelity National Title Co., Fidelity National Title Company of California, Joycie Mack, and Vickie Mickle was filed on 3/2/06.
Dismissal as to Co-defendant Sunnie Richardson will be filed by plaintiff.
Co-defendant: Gary Bell is in settlement with the plaintiff by way of payment. Final payment due in September 2006.
Exhibit 8-17 were ID and admitted into evidence.
Exhibit 2, 4-6, 20, 21, 25-27 were ID and admitted into evidence.
After hearing the testimonies and the evidence presented, the matter was submitted and taken under submission.
Counsel will submit a proposed Judgment directly to Dept. 218.

May 2, 2006
Judgment granted and signed by Comm. Kaplan.
See scanned doc. for break down of the Judgment.

Off Calendar Mar-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David H. Schwartz for plaintiff and moving party First Congregation Church of America Samoa

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED RE MOTION TO COMPEL.
Plaintiff First Congregational Church of American Samoa's motion to compel attendance at deposition is granted in part. The Court orders that defendant contact counsel for plaintiff forthwith to schedule his deposition and production of documents. The deposition shall be completed by March 21, 2006. The Court finds that defendant previously failed to appear and make a reasonable effort to informally resolve the matter. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $577.85 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
The motion for terminating sanctions is continued to March 24, 2006 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 610. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
